data.title: stat -f %m macOS syntax causes crashes on Linux in 5 scripts
data.description: The stat -f %m pattern (macOS file modification time) is used in 5 scripts and causes unbound variable errors on Linux where stat -f means 'filesystem mode'. Affected files: plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh (line 71), plugins/dso/scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh, plugins/dso/scripts/health-check.sh, plugins/dso/hooks/track-cascade-failures.sh, plugins/dso/skills/ui-discover/lock.sh. On Linux, stat -f %m interprets %m as a filename, not a format specifier. With set -u (nounset), the unexpected stdout output triggers unbound variable errors in arithmetic contexts. Fix: use portable pattern: stat -c %Y file 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m file 2>/dev/null (Linux first, macOS fallback).
